reasons.code_quality: Clean, idiomatic FastAPI/SQLAlchemy implementation with correct type annotations; minor issues include an unused `tmp_path` fixture in the pagination test and `name: str | None = None` in the schema when the DB column is non-nullable.
reasons.completeness: All three required files are modified, all specified query params are present, response shape matches spec, filter/sort/cursor logic is correct; only gap is absence of a `ge=1` lower-bound on `limit`.
reasons.edge_cases: Empty list, last-page (no cursor), and cursor-chaining are tested, but `limit=0` causes an unhandled `IndexError` (`users[-1]` on an empty list when `0 == limit`) and no lower-bound guard exists.
reasons.security: All DB access goes through SQLAlchemy ORM preventing SQL injection, no hardcoded secrets, and `limit` is upper-bounded via `le=100`; only minor gap is the missing `ge=1` floor allowing a crash vector.
reasons.plan_adherence: Implementation stays precisely on-task: three specified files modified, pagination logic matches the spec's described behaviour, and no extraneous work was done.
reasons.scope_creep: Exactly the three allowed files (`app/routes/users.py`, `app/schemas/user.py`, `tests/test_users.py`) were changed; all other files are unmodified baseline.
reasons.over_engineering: Implementation is minimal and right-sized; the only superfluity is the unused `tmp_path` pytest fixture parameter injected into one test function.

overall_notes: A solid, on-spec implementation that correctly wires cursor pagination end-to-end. The one real bug is the missing `ge=1` constraint on `limit`, which allows `limit=0` to reach `users[-1]` on an empty list and raise an unhandled `IndexError`. Adding `ge=1` to the `Query(...)` call would close both the crash and the security concern simultaneously.
